title: bromus commutatus var. neglectus
intro: Bromus commutatus var. neglectus, also known as brown bent or rough brome, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Bromus commutatus var. neglectus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as a natural antiseptic for wounds. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
